topic: Two strangers realize they've been dating the same person
raw_output:
[story 1]

The lukewarm coffee tasted like ash in my mouth. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet cafe. Across the small, scarred wooden table, Sarah was staring at me with an expression of utter disbelief. "Wait," she started, her voice tight. "You know Mark?" I blinked, a slow, deliberate movement. "Know him? I... I don't know anyone named Mark." My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck corded. I leaned back, crossing my arms so tightly my knuckles turned white. "Are you serious right now?" The sheer absurdity of the situation made my chest feel incredibly tight. "I've never met anyone named Mark. This is a complete fabrication."

[story 2]

My hands were shaking as I gripped the edge of the counter, the ceramic digging into my palms. Across the room, across the crowded bookstore, Clara looked up, her eyes wide and searching. "Excuse me," she called out, her tone laced with something sharp and wounded. "Did you happen to see a man with a bright blue scarf? He looked incredibly familiar." I straightened up, my gaze locking onto hers, a sudden, hot flush rising up my neck. The air around me seemed to vibrate with suppressed frustration. "I don't know anyone with a blue scarf," I retorted, my voice pitched low, edged with a distinct impatience. I pushed my chair back, the scrape of wood against tile sounding aggressive. "You must be mistaken."

[story 3]

I watched him approach, a familiar silhouette against the afternoon sun. When he turned, his smile was exactly the one I’d been anticipating—and yet, it landed like a physical blow. "Hey," he said, his voice warm. I felt a sudden, intense pressure behind my eyes. I forced a laugh that sounded brittle and thin. "Oh, hello," I managed, my tone deliberately flat, every word measured and cold. I stood up abruptly, needing distance. "We haven't met," I stated, not bothering to look at him as I walked past, my stride too quick, my shoulders hiked up near my ears. The feeling churning in my gut demanded I just get away from this shared history.
